text:
I still can't ever get past when I was just a freshman high school and PanterA changed my life FOREVER!!!! I mean ugh having to grow K-8th in Baptist School but never fitting in as I just never bought into any of the popular trends, or felt the void filled! Then one day 2002 in September This Music Video came on middle of the night and I saw and HEARD the greatest just EVERYTHING! And I was just His solo on this song I imagine it had the same effect on so many others! But I had to get Vulgar Display Of Power! And I finally had that void not just filled but sealed with PRIMAL CONCRETE SLEDGE! And I just while too young to have seen PanterA live, I was getting my news from the metal Magazines! And then as Damageplan was getting up going Darrell and Vinnie Hosted this show and I was just...


Two brothers, Two DemiGods, Two of the greatest Musicians and well Who didn't have Darrell and Vinnie somewhere on their wall growing up! These two brothers were bigger than any others! The Abbotts even put those Romulus&Remus to shame as Power Groove is so beyond words! 

I got tickets to see Damageplan and while so young so excited! When I arose December 9th, 2004 I was in shock as details hadn't been completely out but at first when I learned It was a just... No words, I know everyone was the same as it just couldn't be! But It just 20 years... I only December 8th as a day of just celebrating the Life and Impact And the Fun Dime brought to our world! Dime and Vinnie are Eternal!!! THANK YOU FOR IGNITING THE FIRE IN ME, FOR BEING A MASSIVE INFLUENCE ON RAISING ME, AND FOR EVERYTHING! I along with every last one you both turned into another Cowboy From Hell... I know you both are together and the descendants you left are in the millions! Everyone who also is a COWBOY FROM HELL in unity... We are bonded through this perfection of Power Groove Metal! Everyone stay safe this holiday season and Getcha Pull for how These Brothers changed your life! Thank you so so much!!! \m/ forever!

text:
Jesus is the way, the truth, and the life. Maybe you came to this video just like me to let out all your anger, sadness and anguish. But there is only one who can ease your inner sadness, heal your anger and anguish in your soul, his name is Jesus Christ. He did this to me, I loved Rock in general, I felt welcomed, I felt like my stress was going away and that music would heal my pain, but it never went away except when I learned about Jesus Christ and his word. Come to him, seek while you can find him, he is your creator, the emptiness of your soul is his size. Jesus is immeasurable, love, the true peace of Spirit you are looking for, he is the Son of God, look for him and you will find him.

Luke 19: 10 For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.
____

Jesus é o caminho, a verdade, e a vida. Talvez você tenha vindo nesse vídeo assim como eu colocar pra fora toda sua raiva, tristeza e angústia. Mas só há um que pode aliviar sua tristeza interna, curar sua raiva e angústia na alma, o seu nome é Jesus Cristo. Ele fez isso comigo, eu amava Rock em geral, me sentia acolhido, sentia que meu estresse ia embora e que a música iria curar a minha dor, mas nunca foi embora exceto quando aprendi sobre Jesus Cristo e a sua palavra. Venha para ele, busque ainda enquanto se pode acha-lo, ele é o seu criador, o vazio da sua alma tem o tamanho dele. Jesus é imensurável, amor, a verdadeira paz de Espírito que você procura, ele é o Filho de Deus, procure-o e você o achará.

Lucas 19:10 Porque o Filho do homem veio buscar e salvar o que se havia perdido.

text:
Okay 👌🏻! I will always be a true fan.  And back in the day…  your lyrics kept me company when I lived out on the streets. 
Now it just keeps me company and wishing I could damn well turn a clock back and do it all over again.  Lucky to still be alive.  Trust me on that one. ANYhow, I am 58yrs young and turn up my love for pantera and shred my air guitar 🎸 a oh no forgive me.. haha my neighbors don’t like your style. Hell, they don’t even like most good music.
But hey it’s daylight hours and I can have somewhat of a pretty good loud sound coming from my house out in the country. Nuttin’ they can do about it. Oh no, I sound so cruel don’t I?. So yeah, I love you guys man!
Thankyou for being a part of my Rock and Roll gypsy traveling fantasy.  It’s been a long road and y’all have been right there with me all Dah way.
Did I already say that?
Consider it said again! P.S. It really does kinda suck getting older. Just sayin’.

Anselmo, where he was being asked questions ( ...think it even was in a university... ) and he described an incident, where he supposedly saved the life of some mate of him, who got < overdosed >, so that he was given some shot of atropine straight into his myocard by Anselmo, springing back to life that way but... ...I was confused, if it might have been some kind of retroactivism of memory in that you firstly need way more atropine to get a heart back going and then secondly the victim does not spring back to life immediately, this process happening way more slow with a puls,you can perceive again but not more in the inception of that process... ...besides there exists some cinematography, where excactly the same happens and also there you can see the wrongness in the suggestion department medical treatment ( the film is otherwise just horrible to watch so just don't ask and I saw it once in tv some 25 years ago... ...by Quentin Tarantino... ) and I wonder if this being some brilliant but not disturbing example of retroactivism... ...anyway Cowboys from Hell is a song I like very much ( but I don't hear it too often... ...just to not disturb Mama Joana... ) and I will send some retards back to hell finally, slamming the gates of hell shut from the outside they though opened from the inside!

text: The 1st time I ever heard pantera, I was in 7th grade english class, in 1993 or 94, my buddy keith handed me his walkman and was like "dude listen to this".  I started playing it just as everyone got quiet after the bell, it was on old school walkman headphones, which were literally just speakers, lol, and blaring full volume of course.  It was also playing the intro to A good friend and a bottle of pills, lol the three girls in the row in front of me all whipped their heads around disgusted, everyone within 15 feet could hear it.  And that was my intro to Pantera. . .now I've seen them 5 times. . all with Dime.  Lol.  Who woulda guessed.

text: I was into metal about a decade and had heard a lot of things and of course all the real heavy stuff from the early Slayer, Metallica, Exodus albums when this here suddenly hit my ears. First: this super-clean, super-heavy sound. Second: the new kind of songwriting. Third: the unbelievable groove it had. Blew me away directly. The same named album was bought very quickly and everyone was talking about this band. Still in 2022 especially this song kicks right in your face. Iconic and it will stand the test of time forevermore. Later i saw them several times live on stage. Pure aggression. Greetings from germany.

text:
Pantera was visionary in the music industry and way ahead of their time. Here we are exactly thirty years later, and their music has preserved well.
The sound is as if freshly composed today.
For those unaware, Pantera not only paved the way for mainstream Punk Metal, but also created the sub-genre known today as Groove Metal. 🤘
In my opinion, a sub-genre far greater than either independent genre - metal or punk.
Any 'true' Music Enthusiast of that time  experienced the proverbial, "Whoa!! Who the (beep) was that!?!?!" moment. That is, upon the initial15 sec of hearing a Pantera song for the first time. 

Much love and respect to Phil, Dime, Rex, & Vinnie. 🤘

text: I was in 4th grade (25 now) & one day my friend played the song enter sandman on his blackberry(he was spoiled lol) but his Dad had great taste in music. When I heard this song at lunch off the speakers of his phone it arose a beast in me wanting more & more of this kind of music I had never heard before. I immediately became more and more blown away by their discography & this genre of music turning me on to Pantera,ACDC, Medadeth, Judas priest &  many many more amazing metal bands. The first CD I ever bought was ACDC's Back in Black album & would play it in my parents car, to say the least they were concerned lol.I would lay in bed at night with my headphones, In in the dark & listen to Master of puppets every single night. So glad this was the first genre I ever got into. Have become more & more into metal & it's genres ever since.

text:
I see your doubts, and raise you THROWDOWN. "Venom & Tears" and "Deathless" (last 2 albums are more metal / Pantera-esque, but they always had that vibe)

Songs to check out:

- "Holy Roller" (off of Venom&Tears)

- "BURN" (off of "Vendetta")

- "Forever" (off of "Haymaker")

If you like the heavy / grooving part of Pantera, you might wanna look at the late 90s / early 00s metalcore scene
... 'Plenty of bands with heavy ass riffs, no clean singy-songy vocals. And a lot of those bands are still around, and maybe up your alley;

Unearth (Albums to start with: "The Oncoming Storm" + "III - in the eyes of fire")

Hatebreed (Albums: "Perseverance" + "The Rise of Brutality" + "The Concrete Confessional")

First Blood ("Rules" is my personal fav album) 

Terror - (Albums: "Keepers of the Faith")

And bands like God Forbid, As I Lay Dying; Lamb of God..

All have an air of Pantera (though between them: diff styles) cause the heavyness of bands like Pantera, the riffage of thrash bands like Slayer, and the in-your-face attitude of hardcore punk is what all those bands have in common

Another underground gem that's still around: CONVERGE (one of the most consistently awesome bands in loud ass music, for about 3 decades now)

Oh and Pig Destroyer (if you like grind-esque stuff too)

text: I wish I could post a picture in the comments because I got to see Pantera for free in the late 90s at Arco Arena and go backstage. I got my picture taken with Dimebag. It was the craziest concert I have ever been to in my life! This one drunk guy was walking around screaming and grabbed me and yelled like Ric Flair and then walked into the bathroom. When he came out his pants were down to his ankles and he was still yelling. Some cops tackled him and he kept trying to kick them in the head when he was on the ground. text: I have an interesting story to share with this song : i live in the Philippines and the problem here is they cant keep their favorite songs for themselves they got to play them loud (bon jovi, brian adams, micheal learn to rock (check this danish band cos it s really sh!t)). Whether you have a baby sleeping or you re sick or you just want to sleep they dont give a f. they put volume to  the max. January 2nd 2022, in a hotel /resort a teenage girl started playing loud those tasteless mainstream bands at 6:30 am. Oh god knows how much i hate those songs... so i took my bluetooth speaker put it maximum volume and start playing "Cowboys from Hell" and i checked people faces it was hilarious (in the Philippines most people are not used to heavy guitar riffs). It was like i brought apocalypse to them. Kids started to freak out after 10 seconds, one kid of the noisy family even started to cry (like 6 or 7 years old )... the parents were jaw dropping tjeu were clearly puzzled by the sound. After 1 minute  the grandmother made me signs so i turned off my speaker. I gently smiled to her and asked "is the sound too loud?" she pointed me her grandson and said "he is scared and he is crying". I said "i m sooo sorryyyy. But when your teenage girl put the sound very loud at 6:30am you didn't seem to care that much she might have waken me and my kids up..." Their music stopped on the spot... The kid was still crying while they left their room ... Maybe he was traumatized by Pantera. In fact if they didnt stop the next song was Sepultura (Refuse/Resist) and then for the more resilient the last song that would probably led them to self destruct was Behemoth (Wolves ov Siberia). Lesson learnt...

text:
@benniegeustyn5759  Then she wasnt a Pantera loyalist like me.
I love them equally as much now as I did 30 years ago.
Im not a headbanger and never have I been.
For me it's not about finding a song or band that suits my tastes. 
I heard a song from the album Cowboys from Hell. I dont recall which, but I do recall my reaction. My eyes opened wide with mouth ajar. And I looked up at my friend in shock and didn't utter a word. My ears hadn't heard such a menagerie of sounds.  
Like a kid in a candy store, i said, "Who is THIS????"
It was love thereafter and tho I'm a music fanatic from 1980, Pantera is still my #1 ALL TIME favorite.

text:
​@benniegeustyn5759 I am almost 40 and sometimes I'll headbang to a song like CFH when I'm really digging it. But I have to limit it because I injured my neck. For the longest time, I had a very limited range of motion, like when trying to turn my head to check the blind spots, it was very very difficult. I found a chiropractor and after the first couple of weeks, I had full range of motion in my neck. So I highly recommend finding a chiropractor for any metal heads with a sore neck.
PS: it sounds like Bianca has raised you well 🙂 I totally get the "Just a phase" thing, my sister was like a 1990s version of a hippie when she was a teenager. She wore the flannel and jeans, has peace signs on her bedroom wall, she was really cool. But she eventually grew out of that phase. Now almost 3 decades later, she is happily married with two teenage children of her own.

text: @duffman18  Agreed, vocal warmups and exercises help immensely, but they aren't exactly or exclusively "opera singer" training (yes, opera singers do this). Any vocal instructor for any style of music will get you in a warmup exercise routine right off the bat. It's not so much the high pitch stuff but the loud growling and screaming that ruins your voice. That's why opera singers can sing just fine night after night for years, but metal singers who scream and growl a lot blow out their voice. The combination of drinking, smoking, NOT warming up, bad technique, and screaming/growling night after night is a recipe for disaster. And Phil is certainly not a "technical" singer. Anyone can sound good in the studio though.

text: One thing that was brilliant about Dime is that he knew the magic of simplicity.  He was a skilled guitar player and could have written riffs far more complex,  but chose to stay simple,   which is almost always more effective.  It takes a good writer to be able to understand that.  I mean,  the walk riff is what,  two notes?  Then you listen to this riff,  Drag the waters Riff,  Broken,  Even Cemetery Gates is not all that complex.   All the riffs were simple, but hard as hell and effective.

Dime saved it all up for his solos. He knew how to get us into that groove, so when the time came that groove would further amplify the incomprehensible precision, speed, and shred of what set him apart from all other guitarists.

text: @Snuffsaid2007  You can do a lot with just that and some nimble fingers- I've recently studied some advanced stuff like synthetic scales and chromatic intermediate chords n' shit and I pretty much learned all modes front to back as soon as I had a guitar, but I'd trade all that for a really good ear. But I actually believe that ear training is a thing too- some people talk about "perfect pitch" as though it's something you're born with, it's like being blonde or something, but I think actually people with that skill actually worked hard to get it or were blessed to grow up in a musical family setting.

Your heart must be sealed with My blood. You must be quick to repent often and seek holiness above all else. Love what is true and just. Be extremely careful what your eyes see and your ears hear. This is guarding your heart, guarding its gate. It is in sealing these portals to the soul, in commanding the spirit to govern or have mastery over the soul, that you overcome. You will then be prepared for My return, and be protected from the evil one.
If you have not already done so, seal the portals to the enemy now! Confess your sins and repent for the midnight hour is here! Bind the access points that have given satan legal rights within your temples, within your body, mind, soul and spirit. Give satan and all his dark kingdom a certificate of divorce and be free from the bondage, and from all the oppression and possession in your bloodline immediately! But My Word is quick, powerful, and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the dividing apart of your soul and spirit, your joints and marrow, as I discern the thoughts and intentions of your heart. Allow Me to write My laws on your heart and you will know truth at last. You will know what is now directly in front of you and the choices that are here for you and your loved ones. Do not wait another moment. Come out of Babylon and make Me King of your heart, the Divine Gatekeeper of your soul.

text:
Greatest song about cowboys ever. No square dancing, no pickup trucks,  no chickens, and no bullshit. Just good old fashioned Texas rock.
Pantera was the greatest metal band to hit the airways. When bands like Pearl Jam, STP, Live, The Toadies, and Beck dominated rock radio, there was no room for any real face melting metal. Maybe Sandman and old Sabbath and the same Randy Rhodes tune over and over.
Enter: Pantera. Balls to the wall metal that could make bands like Slayer sound like easy listening music. Heavy heavy shit that would cut through your soul and kick you in the face. They had no problem getting radio play. Now how the hell does that even happen? Mouth for War, Walk, This Love, and the usual grooves were all over the radio. It was crazy. Maybe it was just Dallas radio, but I doubt it. Pantera was fucking beautiful. Man I miss those guys. 
Enough time has passed to assure us that metal will never be the same.

text: This is the song and video that changed my life. I was always a fan of rock n' roll. I grew up on a steady diet of Aerosmith, Bruce Springsteen, and Bob Seger. I knew about the heavier stuff, and I've been a fan of heavy metal since I was around 10 or so. However! I didn't know it was a lifestyle, you know? This is the band, song, and video, that showed me there was a totally different lifestyle out there for me to dive into. My first tattoo was the "CFH" on my right shoulder. Cowboys From Hell changed my life. Really did. I visited Dime in 2012 in Arlington, just to pay my respects and say goodbye. Dimebag, Vinny, Phil, and Rex changed my life, and they changed it for the better. I don't care who reads this, but Pantera fucking ruled and I can all but guarantee Dime and Randy Rhoads are trading licks in the afterlife right now! CFH!!!!!
author: @josephshepard2962
video_title: Pantera - Cowboys From Hell (Official Music Video) [4K Remaster]

Metallica,Megadeth, Slayer, testament,exodus were the renowned ones in the thrash metal scene.
Anthrax are they deserved to be thrash metal?
But no one can deny the fact that Pantera's great revival from glam metal to groove metal with a dominant trait to attract the audience.

text:
Double Barrel, 12 gauge, can't lock me in your cage!    
I had the privilege to see Pantera live 3 times.   1. West Palm Beach Arena - 1997   2. Sunrise Musical Theater with Slayer opening - 1998  3. Coral Sky Amphitheater Ozzfest 2000.    They are as Phil proclaimed from the stage in WPB in 1997, the "Kings of Metal".     They are to the metalcore bands of today what the Beatles were to rock bands of the 70's and 80's.    They pioneered the way for many of the new greats such as August Burns Red, As I Lay Dying, Parkway Drive, Miss May I, I could go on for days listing them......

text: Three cheers for one of the best fucking metal bands to ever give this planet their grace!!!! I don’t have any one “favorite” band, but I have my favorites for sure (Faith No More, Tool, Clutch, etc), and these guys top that list man! Saw them four times...spectacular fucking performers!!! Not many bands out there can sound exactly like they do in the studio live, and it was all that and more!! The seamless blending of what they did in the studio to impromptu improv made for one HELL of a show, let me tell you what, if I may!! Such a fucking shame I’ll never be able to witness that energy again. It was a helluva thing!!! RIP Dime and Vin, Pantera forever!!! 🤟🏻🤟🏻🤟🏻🤟🏻

text:
@moeburn  the thing about Nirvana was that Curt Cobane was the posterboy for grunge and he absolutely hated fame and did nothing but outrageous antics to get him more attention. Now was he being coy and secretively loving it or did he absolutely hate it? Now to address the grunge/metal conflict. As was metioned metal was on its way out and rock needing resuscitation grunge was what gave it new life. Raw, unpolished, and different ppl saw it as degrading to metal bc the simplistic riffs and arrangements and repetitive vocals that had no substance (imo) 

Yes there's nothing wrong with grunge if you  want to trade in your 1972 454 SS Chevelle for a gas saving Prius.
author: @joetroutt7425
video_title: Pantera - Cowboys From Hell (Official Music Video) [4K Remaster]

text:
As someone who's been a Kurt Cobain fan for 30 years... he was being coy and secretly loving it, I'm almost sure of it.  But he also had low self esteem and didn't feel like he deserved it.  And it helped his brand to hate fame.  He had really good brand and appeal awareness, even if he tried to pretend like he didn't care about it.

But all I'm saying is let people like what they like.  Yeah grunge was simple and not technically impressive, Kurt called it "the nursery rhymes of rock".  I like it.  I like the complex and mind blowing stuff too though.

text: I had a bunch of metal fan friends around this time. The guy that ran our local record shop told me I needed to get this disc. I was completely blown away and I could see this band was about to blow up and be huge. One of my friends called Pantera "that band that sounds like Metallica". I said "WTH are you talking about? This band is new, raw, extreme and original. It has a distictly southern groove. They're like nothing else out there. Metallica is played. This band has a real future."  I got the last laugh.

text: this is a song about the members of the titular bad (pantera) acknowledging simultaneously their southern american heritage , being cowboys, while also acknowledging how their sinful ways that come with the culture of the time of this albums release(1990), such as drugs and violence. text: You fucking said it! I still can’t believe that half of the mighty Pantera are no longer with us. It was hard to imagine that Dime isn’t here and his legacy lives on forever, but now Vinnie’s not here as well, and it hurts just as bad. I often wondered if Pantera would’ve reunited if Dime wasn’t taken from us. It’s not fair that all of the most talented musicians have been taken way to early. As a fan you have this undeniably connection with them, as they have a strong connection with you. Dime and Vinnie Rest In Peace. We love you and miss you guys every day. Pantera Forever!

text: maggotmusic757 Cant speak for the person you responded to but as I am also 40... Cowbows from Hell came out in 1990 and was their first major label debut. I was about 12-13 the first time I heard them. If you were into metal, or music in general, you would just go on into record stores, and pick up any new titles that caught your eye. Sometimes it was a bust and sometimes you got something great. I remember picking up the cassette tape and thinking this looks cool. So yes, 28 years ago, Cowboys from Hell was for sale even in my small midwestern home town at the good old Record Town mall store.

text: I remember when Z Rock outta Texas sent its signal to a small station in Circleville Ohio. I live an hour away but I could pick it up somewhere in my house growing up. I loved waking up to Testament, Exciter, Megadeth, all the metal.  They also played PROUD  TO BE LOUD by Pantera. I sent of 5$ to Texas and they sent it to me on tape. I loved all metal and hard rock anything with an attitude and something righteous to say.When I first heard where they had gone from there, I never looked back. No one touched them in their day. R.E.S.P.E.C.T. -Walk  .

Phil Anselmo was in cahoots with aliens while plotting Dimebag's murder. We already know of the leaked transcript from a phone call between dime and Phil that would prove to be the last they had. It is also known that Phil had convinced Dimebag to get life insurance in 2003. He would have motives to kill him as the band had broken up in 2003 and it was Phil vs the Abbott brothers with Rex being neutral. These are the events that all led up to Phil shooting Dimebag on that fateful night and framing Nathan Gale and then blowing the money that was meant to be for the funeral on heroin instead

Phil is the one behind dimebags death. He needed money for his heroin addiction so shot dimebag and framed Nathan Gale. He then blew the life insurance money on heroin instead of paying for the funeral. He actually convinced dimebag to get life insurance in 2003. A bit suspicious he would convince dime to get life insurance and a year later dime happens to die is it not?

Glamtera was a lie made by posers to trick you into thinking Pantera were false metal

Now we are all aware of the claims that Nathan Gale had shot Dimebag due to anger that Pantera had broken up but consider this, if Nathan Gale was so upset about the break up of Pantera, why would he shoot Dime and risk there never being a reunion? Also placing the blame netirely on Dime when Phil was responsible too. This is a very Phil-centric story that the media is trying to paint. text:
Dimebag's loss was so utterly meaningless.  He was a great dude, an awesome guitar player, an overall good man. 
I had the privilege of seeing him live at some point in time, and I can say that he was legitimately a good dude who cared about his fans. I feel his loss, and all I can say is that he is still remembered and honored every time I pick up my guitar an play his riffs. Long live my man dimebag and his crew. They are all are an inextricable part of my life.

text: +Aaron Robinson LOL... but...why would you even "notice" a Black person being in there? I get that I'm not the typical demographic for metal music, but it is sadly amazing that in 2016, it would still seem "odd" if I were at an Unleash The Archers show. text: +Aaron Robinson You know what? I remember when they got that gig. Just before they left they played Backstage. Phil announced it by saying "We're getting ready to go on tour with the main motherf*****s. Judas Priest." I remember the most useless of information..LOL. Makes me great at Trivial Pursuit and Jeopardy. I have pics of Phil, Dime, Rex and Vinnie from Backstage on my FB page as well as some of the other bands that played backstage. I have a couple of pics of my friend and I at the Basement, outside with the sign in the Background. It wasn't a very flattering pic, so I took it down, just a couple of weeks ago when I was cleaning up my photo albums. Dime was the most sociable one. He used to come out and chat. I remember one time he was talking to some folks in the club one night and he kept saying, "Sho' ya riiiiight." Vinnie came out sometimes, too. Our first time to one of their shows, I asked him why they didn't do "Proud To Be Loud". It was probably my favorite song from the Power Metal CASSETTE... LOL....He told me, "The singer doesn't like to do that song." I looked at him kinda weird, thinking..."the singer"?....I know his name is Phil. I wanted to say....ummm...I know all of your names. My friends and I blast  Power Metal.
author: @trishastargirl
video_title: Pantera - Cowboys From Hell (Official Music Video) [4K Remaster]

text: +Aaron Robinson Phil was the most reclusive, but we ventured Backstage at their first or second show. There were others back there and he was telling some story where he kept saying, "Re-animate me." We thought it was funny. My friend and I were very naive. We had no idea he was probably stoned...LOL. I have to say...I thought Phil was cute back then. I think he didn't want that pretty boy image is why the next time they came back he had been shaved one side of his head. But, anyway when the second set began he started talking about something. You know how he said crazy stuff...(but nothing about White power) back then....He did say, "We don't want nobody in our backstage."...At first I thought he was an arsehole...LOL. Other bands didn't mind taking pics and chatting. NO WE WERE NOT GROUPIES...LOL.  It was just us liking the music and their shows. I think that was the night Doug Pinnick of King'X was in the audience and Phil made a point of asking him to come backstage. Doug is Black, by the way...LOL. I decided not to take it personally, the comment about ppl coming back stage because that was their personal space and they had stuff they wanted to do without uninvited ppl seeing it. LOL. I didn't think he was cute anymore, but I also kept coming to the very frequent shows at Backstage.

text: Born 1970, grew up in the parents Saccharine 60's stuff Ventures, Andy Williams, Simon & Garfunkel, Neil Diamond and such; My First Album was Gene Simmons Single album of KISS.. When I was Six...starting point and take the spectrum between the old school sound that was grounded in personal revelations... This rides on in what we get with stuff like this that tends towards the raw power and rage in righteous retribution. That is what a lot of metal is about, when it isn't a parody of the supernatural or social joke. Now I have so much appreciation of this kind of angry tone...  A Little manic there in response, but you'll parse it out if you care...

text: +TimTamTheTurtleMan Player has more to do with it than the amp. You could try palm muting and using very tight gate, boosting bass a little, cutting mids heavily, slight boost to treble. Play with the eq, listen and learn how each band sculpts the sound. You will need lot of gain, but don't overdo it, especially if you don't use gate. Try what works with your gear and learn how your gear works. You won't sound exactly the same no matter what you do, but you can get quite similar sound. Using amp like Randall RG-100 helps, that's what he used.

text:
General Hades You are truly disconnected from reality. Phil was never into the Glam scene. Sure Pantera had spandex and for a while they had big hairdos but so did everyone back then. Look at old pictures of Anthrax, Sepultura, Slayer, Metallica, etc. and you'll see they all wore that style. Hell, Kirk Hammett still wears spandex pants. That was just the Metal style back then and all the bands that Pantera admired back in the day looked like that so that is where they got their style; KISS, Van Halen, Iron Maiden all had big hair and spandex pants. Even Heavy Metal pioneer, Ozzy Osbourne wore makeup, had big hair, and looked like a woman in the 80's.  It's not like Pantera wore makeup and dressed like girls like Poison for example. Besides, when Phil came along Pantera's music changed and they got heavier. Power Metal is an awesome album.
That aside, Phil is the real deal and knows how to fight, doesn't take shit from anyone, and would most likely make you his bitch.
Aside from all that, Pantera, Slayer, and Metallica were the most influential Heavy Metal bands of their time. Pantera's groove sound is what influenced the whole Nu-Metal scene according to Nu-Metal bands themselves. Even already established metal acts like Anthrax and Slayer admitted that Pantera's sound and success is what influenced them to experiment with a new sound on some of their albums.
author: @jameslandon4126

text:
The Golden Age of Metal. Pantera, Slayer, so many others. It's like the 90s were *made* for metal, they learned everything they could from the 80s and 70s and made it even better. Even the bands that were heavily influenced by earlier metal and didn't want to play metal per se just made another new badass genre -- grunge -- re: Soundgarden, Alice in Chains, etc. 

This will always be one of my favorite albums ever, even though I don't listen to metal anymore.

text: i live in a small town, and i raised myself in heavy metal. And the older boys, who now are 30 years old, always told me the legend, the night when Pantera came to town. All the chairs in the velodrome were rooted out and thrown, the doors smashed, several people was injured.
